nodes.label: P2.6 — the substrate filter + the continual veto (closes Phase 2)
nodes.description: Re-runs the boosted-read recipe (N=4 blocks, k=1, P2.1 healthy cell) through the class-incremental digits stream (exp4 setup) vs single-block Phase-1 baseline, GD-online catastrophic baseline, and a no-sleep rot control. 3 seeds.

nodes.label: SUBSTRATE table (recipe -> chip cost)
nodes.description: per-block SCFF = weight crossbar, per-sample norm (no batch/running stats), backward credit=0, online-feasible + continual-safe (no batch stats to rot). GD readouts = small head matrices, online + sleep-consolidated. write mode rejected on both cost and accuracy axes.

nodes.label: P2.6 verdict: VETO PASSED — Phase 2 CLOSED
nodes.description: boosted-read+sleep final 0.932 (BWT -0.034) vs single-block 0.938 (BWT -0.026) vs GD-online 0.186 (BWT -0.992) vs no-sleep 0.332 (BWT -0.802). SCFF all-class probe flat (0.935->0.931, doesn't forget). Honest caveat: multi-block drift compounds slightly (-0.008 BWT vs single-block) — real but negligible.

nodes.label: surviving recipe: [SCFF x k -> GD-readout] x N (read, few blocks)
nodes.description: The Phase-2 deliverable: forward-only single-sample per-sample-normalized SCFF bulk (0 backward) + tiny sleep-consolidated GD readout heads (~17% of full-backprop weight), boosted via logit-sum (read), never write. Continual-safe by construction (no batch statistics anywhere).
nodes.rationale: This is what Phase 3 inherits as the bench and what Phase 4 characterizes.

nodes.label: Decision: Phase 2 closes; recipe hands off to Phase 3
nodes.description: Depth = block-count not SCFF-layer-count. Drift is measured (compounds slightly with blocks) but not built into a gate — that is deferred to the maintenance phase. Hands off the drift measurement and the boosted-read recipe as the reference cell.

hyperedges.description: P2.5 proves the boosted-read recipe earns depth cheaply (static axis, ~85% of GD accuracy at ~1/6 backward cost) and P2.6 proves it preserves the Phase-1 continual win (BWT -0.034 vs single-block -0.026) -- jointly certifying the recipe that closes Phase 2 and becomes the Phase-3 baseline.
